rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Requesting Relief From The Order Of The Court Dismissing Complaint Against Donald Felton (Deceased) Filed By And Through His Personal Representative Mark J Felton
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS NOTICE OF MOTION REQUESTING RELIEF FROM THE ORDER OF THE COURT DISMISSING COMPLAINT AGAINST DONALD FELTON (DECEASED) FILED BY AND THROUGH HIS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE MARK J. FELTON is DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of And Motion Of Ccp 473(B) Attorney Affidavit Or Excusable Mistake, Surprise, Inadvertance To Vacate Order Of Dismissal Issued March 19, 2021 On Defendant Ma?S Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF CCP 473(B) ATTORNEY AFFIDAVIT OR EXCUSABLE MISTAKE, SURPRISE, INADVERTANCE TO VACATE ORDER OF DISMISSAL ISSUED MARCH 19, 2021 ON DEFENDANT MA'S MOTION is DENIED. Plaintiff's Motion appears to be seeking to vacate "March 19, 2021 order for dismissal of Defendant Ma" (Herrera Declaration, paragraph 1). There was no hearing on any motion to dismiss on March 19, 2021 and no order for dismissal was entered on that date. The only motions on calendar for March 19, 2021 were Plaintiff's Motions for Leave to Amend not addressed in this motion. The only orders entered on that date were orders re: Motions for Leave to Amend. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Partition Referee Amy Harringtons Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Partition Referees Scope Of Authority
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2021 line 5. PARTITION REFEREE AMY HARRINGTON'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONFIRM PARTITION REFEREE'S SCOPE OF AUTHORITY is GRANTED in part. Referee's scope of authority is as stated in the order appointing the referee. This authority to sell the property will become effective upon entry of judgment for partition. Order appointing a referee having been sought and obtained by Defendant via a motion in March 2021, all costs associated with appointment of referee and costs incurred by referee from the date of appointment to the date of entry of judgment for partition will be attributed to the Defendant in the final judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
